entidades_recuperadas.texto:
Concept: Class
Ontology: brick.ttl (EXTENSION)
URI: https://w3id.org/rec#Architecture
Label: Architecture
Definition: A designed/landscaped (or potentially designed/landscaped) part of the physical world that has a 3D spatial extent. E.g., a building site, a building, levels within the building, rooms, etc.

Concept: Class
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#Zone
Label: منطقه
Definition: 区域是物理世界或虚拟世界的一部分，既位于现实空间中又可在3D空间中拓展。bot:Zone的子集包括bot:Site, bot:Building, bot:Storey, or bot:Space。bot:Zone实例可包含其他bot:Zone实例，从而可以对区域进行分组或细分。 同时，bot：Zone的实例也可以与其他bot：Zone实例相邻。最后，bot：Zone可以实例化为bot：Element的两个关系，它们可以包含在（bot：containsElement）中，或者与其相邻（bot：adjacentElement）。

Concept: Class
Ontology: dbo.rdf (CORE)
URI: http://www.google.com/digitalbuildings/0.0.1/subfields#Volume
Label: Volume
Definition: The quantity of three-dimensional space contained by a closed surface.

Concept: Property
Ontology: brick.ttl (EXTENSION)
URI: https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#volume
Label: Volume
Definition: Entity has 3-dimensional volume

Concept: Class
Ontology: cis_2016-03-30.n3 (CORE)
URI: http://dati.beniculturali.it/cis/CulturalInstituteOrSite
Label: Cultural Institute or Site
Definition: The class Cultural Institute or Site represents museums, libraries, archive holders, archaeologic areas and parks, monumental areas. The cultural institutes of sites that are owned by public bodies are meant to offer public services. The cultural institutes or sites that are owned by private subjects, and are open to the public, offer a public and societal service.

Concept: Class
Ontology: facility.ttl (EXTENSION)
URI: https://cogito.iot.linkeddata.es/def/facility#Storey
Label: Storey
Definition: A subclass of bot:Storey and, as such, is contained (bot:hasStorey) in one facility:Building, and is intended to contain (bot:hasSpace) one or more facility:Space that are horizontally connected.

Concept: Class
Ontology: building.ttl (CORE)
URI: http://bimerr.iot.linkeddata.es/def/building#Storey
Label: Storey
Definition: The building storey has an elevation and typically represents a (nearly) horizontal aggregation of spaces that are vertically bound.

URI: https://w3id.org/bot#hasStorey
Label: has storey
Definition: Relation to storeys contained in a zone.

Concept: Class
Ontology: building.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#Storey
Label: Storey
Definition: A level part of a building. Concept from the BOT ontology.

Concept: Property
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#hasSpace

Spaces may be contained in storeys, buil.keywords_usadas:
Based on the user's request, I've extracted the key concepts and generated an optimized search list. Here it is:

Spaces, Storeys, Buildings, Construction Sites, Contained, Architecture, Real Estate, Civil Engineering, Spatial Relationships, Ontology, Semantic Web.

Here's a breakdown of the steps I followed:

1. **Conceptos Nucleares:** The main nouns and verbs are:
	* Spaces
	* Storeys
	* Buildings
	* Construction Sites
	* Contained (verb)
2. **Normalización Ontológica:**
	* Category: Spatial Entities
	* Class: Built Environment
	* Type: Architectural Features
3. **Sinónimos Técnicos:**
	* Spaces: Areas, Zones, Regions
	* Storeys: Floors, Levels, Stories
	* Buildings: Structures, Edifices, Constructions
	* Construction Sites: Building Sites, Development Areas, Infrastructure Projects

The resulting list is a combination of these concepts, normalized and with technical synonyms:

Spaces, Storeys, Buildings, Construction Sites, Contained, Architecture, Real Estate, Civil Engineering, Spatial Relationships, Ontology, Semantic Web.

entidades_recuperadas.texto:
Concept: Property
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#hasElement
Label: 含构件
Definition: Links a Zone to an Element that is either contained in, adjacent to or intersecting with the Zone. The intended use of this relationship is not to be stated explicitly, but to be inferred from its sub-properties. It will, for example, allow one to query for all the doors of a building given that they have an adjacency to spaces of the building.

Concept: Property
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#intersectingElement
Label: intersecting element
Definition: رابطه بین یک منطقه با المان ساختمانی که با آن در تلاقی است.

Concept: Property
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#adjacentElement
Label: 相邻构件
Definition: Relation between a zone and its adjacent building elements, bounding the zone.

Concept: Property
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#hasZeroPoint
Label: 含有原点
Definition: Links a bot:Site to an instance that encodes the latitude and longitude of the Zero Point of the building site. This could be an instance of a wgs84:Point. The definition of GIS and geometry is not within the scope of BOT and an appropriate ontology needs to be selected here by the user. The use of this property is potentially ambiguous and it might be removed or revised in future editions of the ontology.

Concept: Property
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#intersectsZone
Label: intersects zone
Definition: رابطه بین دو منطقه که حدود سه بعدی آنها با هم تلاقی دارد. برای مثال، یک راه پله از منطقه های مختلف عبور می کند و با آنها تلاقی دارد.

brick.ttl: Construction sites are areas with spatial 3D volumes
dbo.rdf: Construction sites are areas with spatial 3D volumes
bot.ttl: Spaces may be contained in storeys, buildings, and construction sites
Spaces may intersect different storeys, buildings, and construction sites
odrl_2017-09-16.n3: Being able to assign multiple individuals of type Party to a Group for which permissions/prohibitions/duties can be specified.
dpv_2025-08-01.n3: Being able to assign multiple individuals of type Party to a Group for which permissions/prohibitions/duties can be specified.

veredicto_final:
**Veredicto Final**

Recomendación de Red: **brick.ttl**, **bot.ttl**, and **facility.ttl**

Justificación:

1. **brick.ttl**: This ontology covers the concept of construction sites as spatial 3D volumes, which is a fundamental aspect of our domain. It provides a solid foundation for understanding the physical environment.
2. **bot.ttl**: This ontology focuses on spaces, storeys, buildings, and construction sites, providing a comprehensive framework for describing spatial relationships and containment. Its intersection capabilities allow us to model complex scenarios.
3. **facility.ttl**: This ontology complements the previous two by introducing the concept of storeys containing spaces, which is crucial for understanding the internal structure of buildings.

Observaciones de Cobertura y Posibles Huecos:

* The recommended red covers a significant portion of the original matrix, including spatial concepts (brick.ttl and bot.ttl) and building structures (facility.ttl).
* The absence of dbo.rdf and odrl_2017-09-16.n3/dpv_2025-08-01.n3 from the recommended red is due to their focus on Party management and permissions, which seem less relevant to our domain's core concerns.
* Although dpv_2025-08-01.n3 is not included in the recommendation, its concepts might be indirectly addressed through the bot.ttl ontology's ability to model containment relationships.

By combining these three ontologies, we can create a robust framework for describing construction sites, buildings, and their internal structures, while also providing a foundation for modeling spatial relationships and containment.
